noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of L'Oréal's information and technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits L'Oréal best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits L'Oréal's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: The AI program at L'Oréal sits inside a global portfolio of brands, markets, labs, factories, salons, retailers and digital channels supported by thousands of digital, technology and data professionals. In that setting, a frontier model has to be useful across knowledge work and software delivery without creating an unmanageable decision surface. Anthropic gives the information and technology organization one platform for reasoning over consumer profiles, product formulations, ingredient data, skin and hair signals, campaign assets, commerce activity and research results, orchestrating bounded agents and assisting development across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications. Claude's focus on steerability and predictable behavior is especially relevant where privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation cannot be bolted on later. The result would be a safer path from isolated copilots to personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data.
cases: Reason across consumer profiles, product formulations, ingredient data, skin and hair signals, campaign assets, commerce activity and research results without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: L'Oréal's scientists, formulators, marketers, commerce teams, beauty advisers, engineers and manufacturing staff generate valuable context through beauty consultations, salon feedback, lab notes, consumer research and field observations, but much of it never reaches a structured system because typing is slow or happens after the fact. Wispr Flow matters as a voice input layer that works inside the applications people already use, turning natural speech into polished text and commands. For L'Oréal, it could capture observations directly into R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications without introducing another destination application. The result is a cleaner human decision trace, faster documentation and better data for the workflows behind personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data.
cases: Turn beauty consultations, salon feedback, lab notes, consumer research and field observations into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: For L'Oréal, browser automation is a practical way to connect modern agents with retailer portals, social platforms, marketplace consoles, regulatory sites and regional brand tools before every dependency is rebuilt or exposed through an API. Browserbase provides the secure, programmable sessions, scaling and observability needed to make that connection production-ready. Agents could complete narrow steps across insight-to-formulation, content-to-commerce and consultation-to-recommendation workflows, while engineering teams use the same infrastructure to validate R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications under realistic conditions. The strategic value is less manual web work without accepting an unmanaged collection of scripts, stored credentials and opaque browser sessions.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across retailer portals, social platforms, marketplace consoles, regulatory sites and regional brand tools without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Developer productivity at L'Oréal is constrained when setup, hardware and local configuration differ across the teams building R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications. Coder replaces that variability with centrally configured cloud development environments that can be provisioned from policy and shut down when not in use. The model is relevant to privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation because code, credentials and regulated tools remain inside the enterprise boundary. It also gives new engineers and acquired or distributed teams a consistent starting point, shortening the path from access request to a productive, compliant workspace.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As L'Oréal adds agents to insight-to-formulation, content-to-commerce and consultation-to-recommendation workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find commerce services, lab systems, marketing automations, integration accounts and AI agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help L'Oréal scale automation while keeping the blast radius around consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory commerce services, lab systems, marketing automations, integration accounts and AI agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: L'Oréal has practical multimodal needs across product imagery, virtual try-on, campaign video, synthetic beauty content and diagnostic visuals. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The information and technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new formulation, content, commerce, diagnostic and adviser experiences.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: L'Oréal's data platform has to support its current agenda—scaling a global Beauty Tech platform across agentic commerce, generative content, computational formulation, virtual try-on and personalized beauty services—and remain trusted continuously, not only after a dashboard or agent produces a bad answer. Monte Carlo can monitor consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data from ingestion through transformation, model and agent behavior, connecting a failure to the systems and teams affected. That gives scientists, formulators, marketers, commerce teams, beauty advisers, engineers and manufacturing staff a faster way to isolate incidents and prevent recurrence. For the information and technology organization, the value is a single trust plane across traditional analytics and emerging agentic workflows, reducing the gap between data quality, model reliability and business impact.
cases: Monitor consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data.

tonic.pull: The teams building R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications need realistic data, but using production copies of consumer, biometric, image, formulation, clinical and commerce data creates unnecessary exposure and slows access approvals. Tonic matters because it can generate synthetic, de-identified datasets that preserve the shape and utility of enterprise data for development, testing and AI work. For L'Oréal, that would let engineers reproduce edge cases, train or evaluate models and refresh lower environments without moving identifiable records into places they do not belong. The result is faster delivery with fewer sensitive records outside production—important in an environment shaped by privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation.
cases: Replace production copies of consumer, biometric, image, formulation, clinical and commerce data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: L'Oréal depends on labs, factories, stores, cloud platforms, regional offices and partner networks, but automation is only as reliable as the inventory and configuration data behind it. NetBox Labs matters as a source of truth for documenting, managing and automating IT and network infrastructure. It could give the information and technology organization one current model of sites, devices, circuits, addresses and dependencies across that estate. That foundation makes changes to global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries safer, improves incident context and gives infrastructure agents a trusted state to read before they act. It replaces stale spreadsheets with governed infrastructure context and supports personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across labs, factories, stores, cloud platforms, regional offices and partner networks.
Drive provisioning and change validation for global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: For L'Oréal, the highest-value AI workloads sit close to consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data and often need bursty batch or agentic execution. Valar provides an in-VPC inference platform built for that pattern, using scheduling, routing and accelerator placement to turn installed compute into more usable capacity. It would give platform teams one operating layer for formulation science, content creation, personalization, commerce and beauty-adviser agents, while prompts, outputs and telemetry remain within the enterprise boundary. That makes Valar a strong fit where external model traffic is constrained by privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation, but the business still needs production-grade throughput.
cases: Run formulation science, content creation, personalization, commerce and beauty-adviser agents inside L'Oréal's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: Software supply-chain risk at L'Oréal begins with what applications and agents are built on. Echo provides CVE-free, maintained alternatives for container bases, libraries, VMs, Helm charts and end-of-life components, removing known vulnerability work at the source. Applied to R&D, commerce, content, diagnostic, manufacturing and AI-platform applications, it would let engineering and security teams spend less time patching the same underlying package across many products. That supports personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data while strengthening the evidence around GDPR, EU AI rules, product claims, cosmetics regulation, biometric consent and brand governance, particularly for workloads that need hardened or FIPS-aligned components.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across R&D, commerce, content, diagnostic, manufacturing and AI-platform applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: L'Oréal's current agenda—scaling a global Beauty Tech platform across agentic commerce, generative content, computational formulation, virtual try-on and personalized beauty services—will increase the number of connections among applications, data services and AI agents faster than traditional firewall review can keep pace. Act Security maps those relationships and highlights communication that is unnecessary or inconsistent with business purpose. Applied to global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries, it could guide safe, incremental perimeter changes around the systems handling consumer, product, formulation, ingredient, image, marketing and commerce data. The result is enforceable least privilege that reflects the real communication paths behind insight-to-formulation, content-to-commerce and consultation-to-recommendation workflows, rather than a static policy that becomes obsolete after the next release.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries.
Create least-privilege perimeters around R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: As L'Oréal expands R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications, security decisions need to move upstream into the planning of new formulation, content, commerce, diagnostic and adviser experiences. Clover Security matters because it brings product-security context into the tools where requirements and designs are already being discussed, using sources such as Jira, Confluence and Drive to identify risk before implementation. That would help scientists, formulators, marketers, commerce teams, beauty advisers, engineers and manufacturing staff receive concrete security guidance while changes are still inexpensive to make. The result is a design process that treats privacy, biometric sensitivity, product safety, claims accuracy and regional regulation as an input to product work rather than a late release gate.
cases: Review new formulation, content, commerce, diagnostic and adviser experiences while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: L'Oréal has to prove trust across GDPR, EU AI rules, product claims, cosmetics regulation, biometric consent and brand governance while its technology estate and third-party relationships continue to change. Drata matters because it turns controls, evidence, risks and assurance signals into a continuously monitored trust graph rather than a periodic collection exercise. The information and technology organization could use it to connect policies and owners to the systems behind R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications, surface control drift and reuse evidence across overlapping reviews. That makes compliance more operational and gives leadership a current view of the risks that could slow progress toward personalized beauty innovation at global scale with responsible use of consumer and scientific data.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries.
Reuse assurance work across GDPR, EU AI rules, product claims, cosmetics regulation, biometric consent and brand governance instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: The cloud-security problem at L'Oréal is not a shortage of findings; it is knowing which combinations of exposure, identity, vulnerability and data actually matter across global cloud, lab, factory and brand workloads with regional data and regulatory boundaries. Orca Security connects those signals in one agentless platform and guides remediation by business context. For the information and technology organization, that can reduce coverage gaps across R&D, formulation, commerce, content, virtual-try-on, manufacturing and brand applications and emerging AI workloads while avoiding host-agent overhead. The value is a clearer, faster path from a cloud risk to the owner and action required to protect insight-to-formulation, content-to-commerce and consultation-to-recommendation workflows.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across L'Oréal's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive L'Oréal data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
